title: Review this uncommitted diff for bugs, security issues, and code quality. Be concise (max 10 lines).
summary_text: - High: [scheduler.py:504](/Users/masayuki/Dev/x-auto/scheduler.py#L504) only detects `"twitter.com/status/"`, but real status URLs are usually `twitter.com/<user>/status/<id>`. Those posts now miss the quote-tweet branch and can incorrectly go out as image posts instead of quote posts. - Medium: [scheduler.py:509](/Users/masayuki/Dev/x-auto/scheduler.py#L509), [x_poster.py:69](/Users/masayuki/Dev/x-auto/x_poster.py#L69) can turn a URL-only quote post into `clean_text == ""`, then `post_tweet()` raises `ValueError("Tweet text cannot be empty.")`. That is a behavioral regression from the previous URL-in-body path. - No obvious new security issue in this diff. - Residual risk: no test coverage here for quote-URL parsing variants (`twitter.com/<user>/status`, `x.com/i/web/status`, URL-only posts), so this path is brittle until those cases are exercised.
